# Building Event-Driven Microservices (Adam Bellemare)

Before writing, modifying, or planning any code, you MUST use your `read_file` tool to load the relevant guidelines from `.roo/vendor/`. You may read multiple files if the task crosses domains.

## Table of Contents:
    - [Why Event-Driven Microservices](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-event-driven-microservices-adam-bellemare/01-why-event-driven-microservices.md): The problem space that a business occupies and provides solutions to, existing regardless of the business itself.
    - [Event-Driven Microservice Fundamentals](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-event-driven-microservices-adam-bellemare/02-event-driven-microservice-fundamentals.md): The design, architecture, implementation, review, or debugging of Event-Driven Microservices (EDMs), event streams, event schemas, stream-processing topologies, state materialization, or the integration of containerized event-driven applications with event brokers.
    - [Communication and Data Contracts](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-event-driven-microservices-adam-bellemare/03-communication-and-data-contracts.md): Designing, implementing, evaluating, or modifying event structures, data contracts, schemas, event streams, payload serializations, or cross-service communication layers within an event-driven architecture.
    - [Integrating Event-Driven Architectures with Existing Systems](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-event-driven-microservices-adam-bellemare/04-integrating-event-driven-architectures-with-existing-systems.md): Integrating legacy systems, monolithic applications, or existing relational databases into an event-driven architecture, including tasks such as data extraction, data liberation, Change-Data Capture (CDC), Outbox table implementation, event sourcing from existing data stores, or sinking event data into external/legacy databases.
    - [Event-Driven Processing Basics](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-event-driven-microservices-adam-bellemare/05-event-driven-processing-basics.md): Designing, writing, reviewing, or modifying stateless event-driven microservices, specifically focusing on event processing topologies, consuming and producing events, applying data transformations (filtering, mapping, routing), branching/merging event streams, and configuring partition assignments, repartitioning, or copartitioning strategies.
    - [Deterministic Stream Processing](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-event-driven-microservices-adam-bellemare/06-deterministic-stream-processing.md): Designing, implementing, refactoring, or reviewing stream processing applications, event-driven microservices, stateful streaming topologies, time-series event processing, windowing functions, or any task involving the synchronization, ordering, or reprocessing of event streams.
    - [Stateful Streaming](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-event-driven-microservices-adam-bellemare/07-stateful-streaming.md): Designing, implementing, configuring, reviewing, or refactoring stateful event-driven microservices, including state materialization, state store selection, state recovery mechanisms, changelog management, schema evolution for state stores, and effectively-once processing logic.
    - [Building Workflows with Microservices](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-event-driven-microservices-adam-bellemare/08-building-workflows-with-microservices.md): Designing, implementing, modifying, evaluating, or debugging multi-microservice workflows, distributed transactions (Sagas), or when deciding between/implementing Choreography versus Orchestration patterns in an event-driven architecture.
    - [Microservices Using Function-as-a-Service](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-event-driven-microservices-adam-bellemare/09-microservices-using-function-as-a-service.md): Designing, implementing, configuring, or debugging Function-as-a-Service (FaaS) solutions, serverless event-driven microservices, cloud functions (e.g., AWS Lambda, Google Cloud Functions, Azure Functions), open-source serverless frameworks (e.g., OpenFaaS, OpenWhisk, Kubeless, Pulsar FaaS), or function-to-function communication topologies within an event-driven architecture.
    - [Basic Producer and Consumer Microservices](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-event-driven-microservices-adam-bellemare/10-basic-producer-and-consumer-microservices.md): The architecting, developing, refactoring, or reviewing of Basic Producer and Consumer (BPC) microservices, including legacy system integration into event-driven architectures, implementation of simple stateless or stateful event processors outside of streaming frameworks, creation of Sidecar data-sinking applications, and coordination of Hybrid BPC applications with external stream processors.
    - [Heavyweight Framework Microservices](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-event-driven-microservices-adam-bellemare/11-heavyweight-framework-microservices.md): Designing, developing, deploying, testing, or troubleshooting microservices using Heavyweight Stream-Processing Frameworks, such as Apache Spark, Apache Flink, Apache Storm, Apache Heron, and Apache Beam, involving big-data stream processing, MapReduce-style topologies, complex windowing/aggregations, cluster-based stream processing, and container-management system (CMS) integration.
    - [Lightweight Framework Microservices](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-event-driven-microservices-adam-bellemare/12-lightweight-framework-microservices.md): Designing, implementing, configuring, scaling, or debugging event-driven microservices that utilize Lightweight Frameworks (e.g., Apache Kafka Streams, Apache Samza in Embedded Mode), involving stream processing topologies, state materialization, stream-table joins, event shuffling via internal streams, and deploying JVM-based stream processors via a Container Management System (CMS).
    - [Integrating Event-Driven and Request-Response Microservices](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-event-driven-microservices-adam-bellemare/13-integrating-event-driven-and-request-response-microservices.md): Designing, implementing, or debugging integrations between asynchronous event-driven microservices (EDMs) and synchronous Request-Response APIs, including handling external analytical events, querying third-party APIs, serving real-time stateful data via REST endpoints, and designing user interfaces (UIs) or micro-frontend applications backed by event-driven architectures.
    - [Supportive Tooling](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-event-driven-microservices-adam-bellemare/14-supportive-tooling.md): DevOps, infrastructure, configuration, scaffolding, monitoring, or security for Event-Driven Microservices (EDMs).
    - [Testing Event-Driven Microservices](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-event-driven-microservices-adam-bellemare/15-testing-event-driven-microservices.md): Creating, updating, configuring, or debugging tests for event-driven microservices.
    - [Deploying Event-Driven Microservices](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-event-driven-microservices-adam-bellemare/16-deploying-event-driven-microservices.md): Creating, modifying, or reviewing deployment scripts, CI/CD pipelines, container management system (CMS) configurations, schema migration strategies, or infrastructure-as-code for event-driven microservices.
